llmResponse.agendaChanges: Dennis McBride requested addition of a 'Staff Recognition' item after Item 5 (Oral Communication). No public comment cards were received for Item 5, so Oral Communication was effectively skipped. The meeting proceeded: Call to Order/Roll Call, Welcome/Pledge, Changes to Agenda, Approval of Agenda, then Staff Recognition (added item), then Item 6 (Action Items — bond consent), then bond action item 7.1 (not on posted agenda but taken up), then Item 8.1 (COVID-19 at-home learning discussion with public comment), then Item 10.1 (Resolution of Emergency re COVID-19 — corresponds to posted agenda Section 6 item), then reports/adjournment. Note: The posted agenda's Section 6 items (Emergency Meeting declaration and Resolution 22) appear to correspond to what the transcript calls items 6, 7.1, 8.1, and 10.1 — this meeting appears to have had a fuller agenda beyond the posted emergency agenda, suggesting a combined regular and emergency meeting.

items.title: Call to Order / Roll Call
Welcome by the School Board President / Pledge of Allegiance
Changes to the Agenda
Approval of Agenda
Oral Communication (for items not on the Regular Board Agenda)
Staff Recognition (added item) / Action Items — Bond Program Consent
Emergency Meeting of the Governing Board — Bond Action Item 7.1 (TAF Construction Agreement)
Resolution 22 — COVID-19 At-Home Learning / District Update (Item 8.1 in transcript)

publicComments.name: Pablo Lopez
publicComments.summary: Asked about the lack of formal special education and preschool distance learning plans and due dates, and raised concern about regression in students with special needs.

publicComments.name: Emily Yonts
publicComments.summary: Submitted written comment asking how after-school programs can support students virtually given that student school emails cannot accept outside addresses.

publicComments.name: Michelle Butler
publicComments.summary: Thanked district staff and leadership, acknowledged the difficulty of serving special education students in distance learning, and expressed hope that teacher dedication would be considered in compensation discussions.

publicComments.name: Unidentified speaker (parent of a second grader / first grade parent)
publicComments.summary: Asked about getting a consolidated list of student login credentials for home use, expressed preference for Zoom over Google Meet, and asked whether out-of-district transfers for Mandarin immersion could resume if the district returns to LCFF funding.

items.title: Resolution 22 — Resolution Designating District Public Works Projects as Essential Governmental Functions (Item 10.1 in transcript)
Adjournment
